name: UNDRIP 2007 (UN Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples)
summary: The United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples (UNDRIP) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-UNDRIP framework adopted by the UN General Assembly — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational framework operating on formal international-indigenous-peoples-rights framework + foundational Indigenous-rights framework alongside ILO Convention 169 framework (UNDRIP adopted 13 September 2007 by UN General Assembly Resolution 61/295 — 144 in favour + 4 against US + Canada + Australia + New Zealand + 11 abstentions + 4-against-states all-reversed-position 2009-2010 + UNDRIP operations began 2007 + UN Permanent Forum on Indigenous Issues UNPFII established 2000 + 25-year mandate framework — formal foundational UNDRIP framework establishing comprehensive UNDRIP organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-treaty frameworks which operate on different substantive areas, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational framework operating on formal international-indigenous-peoples-rights framework + foundational Indigenous-rights framework alongside ILO Convention 169 + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Free Prior and Informed Consent FPIC framework via Article 19 + Article 32 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Self-Determination framework via Article 3 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Family + Children framework via Article 14 + Article 17 + Article 22 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal four-against-states-all-reversed-position 2009-2010 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Canada-Bill-C-15-UNDRIP-Act 2021 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Australia-Federal-Court-rejection 2023 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Trudeau-UNDRIP-Action-Plan 2023 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous Peoples Special Rapporteur framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal UNDRIP-15th-anniversary 2022 + 20th-anniversary 2027 framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal UNDRIP Free Prior and Informed Consent FPIC framework (UNDRIP Article 19 + Article 32 framework providing formal Free Prior and Informed Consent FPIC framework — formal foundational UNDRIP FPIC framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational framework operating formal Free Prior and Informed Consent FPIC framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-veto framework over Indigenous-Lands + Resources + Cultural-Property framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal UNDRIP Indigenous Self-Determination + Indigenous Family + Children framework (UNDRIP Article 3 + Article 14 + Article 17 + Article 22 framework providing formal Indigenous-Self-Determination + Indigenous-Education + Indigenous-Family + Indigenous-Children framework — formal foundational UNDRIP Indigenous-Self-Determination + Indigenous-Family + Children framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Self-Determination framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Family + Children + Sixties-Scoop-redress framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal Four-against-states-all-reversed-position framework (Four-against-states-all-reversed-position framework: Australia reversed 3 April 2009 + New Zealand reversed 19 April 2010 + Canada reversed 12 November 2010 + United States reversed 16 December 2010 — formal foundational Four-against-states-all-reversed-position framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational framework operating formal Four-against-states-all-reversed-position framework). The UNDRIP is operating upon 193 UN member states framework + Indigenous Peoples organizations + national-level Indigenous self-government frameworks — comprehensive universal coverage framework. The Declaration is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal UNDRIP Article 14 + Article 17 + Article 22 framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Indigenous cross-border-family-rights framework + Sixties-Scoop + Indian Residential Schools-redress framework + Indigenous-Family-Court-Jurisdiction framework + Indigenous-Custom-Adoption framework. The UN Permanent Forum on Indigenous Issues UNPFII + Expert Mechanism on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples EMRIP + Special Rapporteur on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples SRRIP are the formal UNDRIP bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The UNDRIP is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: UNDRIP
statutory_framework.relevance: UNDRIP adopted 13 September 2007 by UN General Assembly Resolution 61/295 — formal foundational UNDRIP framework establishing comprehensive UNDRIP organisation framework.

statutory_framework.title: UNDRIP Article 3
statutory_framework.relevance: UNDRIP Article 3 framework on Indigenous Self-Determination — formal foundational UNDRIP Indigenous Self-Determination framework.

statutory_framework.title: UNDRIP Articles 19 + 32
statutory_framework.relevance: UNDRIP Article 19 + Article 32 framework on Free Prior and Informed Consent FPIC — formal foundational UNDRIP FPIC framework.

statutory_framework.title: UNDRIP Articles 14 + 17 + 22
statutory_framework.relevance: UNDRIP Article 14 + Article 17 + Article 22 framework on Indigenous-Education + Indigenous-Family + Indigenous-Children — formal foundational UNDRIP Indigenous-Family + Children framework with PA-intersection framework.

statutory_framework.title: ILO Convention 169
statutory_framework.relevance: ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples adopted 27 June 1989 + effective 5 September 1991 — formal foundational ILO Convention 169 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework on Indigenous Peoples + 24 ratifications as of 2026.

statutory_framework.citation: Canada Bill C-15 United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act 2021
statutory_framework.title: Canada UNDRIP Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Canada Bill C-15 United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act adopted 21 June 2021 — formal foundational Canada UNDRIP Act framework.

apex_courts.name: UN Permanent Forum on Indigenous Issues (UNPFII)
apex_courts.role: Formal UN advisory body for Indigenous Peoples established 28 July 2000 by UN ECOSOC Resolution 2000/22 — first UN agency with Indigenous Peoples expert members.

apex_courts.name: Expert Mechanism on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples (EMRIP)
apex_courts.role: Formal Human Rights Council EMRIP body established 14 December 2007 by HRC Resolution 6/36.

apex_courts.name: Special Rapporteur on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples (SRRIP)
apex_courts.role: Formal Human Rights Council Special Rapporteur on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples body established 24 April 2001 — currently José Francisco Calí Tzay 2020-2025+ framework.

professional_regulators.name: UN Department of Economic and Social Affairs DESA Indigenous Peoples Unit

anonymisation_convention: UNDRIP-related communications operate via state-state framework via UN General Assembly + UNPFII + EMRIP + SRRIP; individual communications limited under UNDRIP framework + formal anonymisation convention under Indigenous Peoples organization protocols.

key_developments.title: ILO Convention 169
key_developments.description: ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples adopted 27 June 1989 + effective 5 September 1991 — predecessor framework to UNDRIP + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework on Indigenous Peoples.

key_developments.title: UNPFII established
key_developments.description: UN Permanent Forum on Indigenous Issues UNPFII established 28 July 2000 by UN ECOSOC Resolution 2000/22 — formal first UN agency with Indigenous Peoples expert members framework operational.

key_developments.title: SRRIP Special Rapporteur on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples established
key_developments.description: Special Rapporteur on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples SRRIP established 24 April 2001 by UN Commission on Human Rights Resolution 2001/57 — formal SRRIP framework operational.

key_developments.title: UNDRIP adopted + EMRIP established
key_developments.description: UNDRIP adopted 13 September 2007 by UN General Assembly Resolution 61/295 — 144 in favour + 4 against US + Canada + Australia + New Zealand + 11 abstentions + EMRIP established 14 December 2007 by HRC Resolution 6/36 — formal foundational UNDRIP + EMRIP framework operational.

key_developments.title: Australia reverses-against-UNDRIP
key_developments.description: Australia reversed against-position on UNDRIP 3 April 2009 — formal Australia-reversed-UNDRIP framework operational.

key_developments.title: New Zealand + Canada + US reverse-against-UNDRIP
key_developments.description: New Zealand reversed 19 April 2010 + Canada reversed 12 November 2010 + United States reversed 16 December 2010 — formal four-against-states-all-reversed-position framework operational.

key_developments.title: World Conference on Indigenous Peoples
key_developments.description: World Conference on Indigenous Peoples held 22-23 September 2014 in New York + Outcome Document adopted 22 September 2014 — formal World Conference on Indigenous Peoples framework operational.

key_developments.title: Canada UNDRIP Act + Indian Residential Schools mass-graves revelations
key_developments.description: Canada Bill C-15 United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act adopted 21 June 2021 + Indian Residential Schools mass-graves revelations 2021 framework — formal Canada UNDRIP Act + IRS-mass-graves framework operational.

key_developments.title: UNDRIP 15th anniversary + UN International Decade of Indigenous Languages 2022-2032
key_developments.description: UNDRIP 15th anniversary commemorated 13 September 2022 + UN International Decade of Indigenous Languages 2022-2032 launched — formal UNDRIP 15th anniversary + Indigenous Languages Decade framework operational.

key_developments.title: Trudeau UNDRIP Action Plan + Australia Voice referendum rejection
key_developments.description: Trudeau UNDRIP Action Plan adopted 21 June 2023 + Australia Voice to Parliament referendum rejected 14 October 2023 — formal Trudeau-UNDRIP-Action-Plan + Australia-Voice-rejection framework operational.

structural_findings: UNDRIP operates a supranational global framework — places UNDRIP in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global supranational framework operating on formal international-indigenous-peoples-rights framework + foundational Indigenous-rights framework alongside ILO Convention 169 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational framework operating on formal international-indigenous-peoples-rights framework + foundational Indigenous-rights framework alongside ILO Convention 169 + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Free Prior and Informed Consent FPIC framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Self-Determination framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Family + Children framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal four-against-states-all-reversed-position 2009-2010 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Canada-Bill-C-15-UNDRIP-Act 2021 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Australia-Voice-rejection 2023 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Trudeau-UNDRIP-Action-Plan 2023 framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous Peoples Special Rapporteur framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal UNDRIP-15th-anniversary 2022 + 20th-anniversary 2027 framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal UNDRIP Free Prior and Informed Consent FPIC framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational framework operating formal Free Prior and Informed Consent FPIC framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-veto framework over Indigenous-Lands + Resources + Cultural-Property framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal UNDRIP Indigenous Self-Determination + Indigenous Family + Children framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Self-Determination framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Family + Children + Sixties-Scoop-redress framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Four-against-states-all-reversed-position framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational framework operating formal Four-against-states-all-reversed-position framework.

193 UN member state ratification framework + Indigenous Peoples organizations + national-level Indigenous self-government frameworks is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive universal coverage framework.

1989 ILO 169 + 2000 UNPFII + 2001 SRRIP + 2007 UNDRIP + EMRIP + 2009-2010 four-against-states-reversed + 2014 World Conference + 2021 Canada UNDRIP Act + IRS-mass-graves + 2022 15th anniversary + Indigenous Languages Decade + 2023 Trudeau Action Plan + Australia Voice rejection UNDRIP-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

UNDRIP + ILO 169 + UNPFII + EMRIP + SRRIP + FPIC + Indigenous Self-Determination + Indigenous Family + Children formal octet framework with formal Indigenous cross-border-family-rights + Sixties-Scoop + Indian Residential Schools-redress + Indigenous-Family-Court-Jurisdiction + Indigenous-Custom-Adoption PA-intersection framework is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: UNDRIP jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global international-indigenous-peoples-rights foundational-UNDRIP meta-framework (UNDRIP 2007 Resolution 61/295 + Article 3 + Articles 19 + 32 FPIC + Articles 14 + 17 + 22 + ILO Convention 169 1989/1991 + UNPFII 2000 + EMRIP 2007 + SRRIP 2001 + Canada UNDRIP Act 2021 + 2009-2010 four-against-states-reversed + 2014 World Conference + 2021 IRS-mass-graves + 2022 15th anniversary + 2023 Trudeau Action Plan + Australia Voice rejection). Only modern global supranational framework operating on formal international-indigenous-peoples-rights framework + foundational Indigenous-rights framework alongside ILO Convention 169 + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Free Prior and Informed Consent FPIC framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Self-Determination framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous-Family + Children framework + only modern global supranational framework operating formal four-against-states-all-reversed-position 2009-2010 + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Canada-Bill-C-15-UNDRIP-Act 2021 + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Australia-Voice-rejection 2023 + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Trudeau-UNDRIP-Action-Plan 2023 + only modern global supranational framework operating formal Indigenous Peoples Special Rapporteur + only modern global supranational framework operating formal UNDRIP-15th-anniversary 2022 + 20th-anniversary 2027 + central jurisdiction of formal FPIC + Indigenous-veto over Indigenous-Lands + Resources + Cultural-Property + central jurisdiction of formal Indigenous-Self-Determination + Indigenous-Family + Children + Sixties-Scoop-redress + central jurisdiction of formal Four-against-states-all-reversed-position + 193 UN member states + UNPFII New York + EMRIP Geneva + SRRIP Geneva + SRRIP José Francisco Calí Tzay 2020-2025 + 1989 ILO 169 + 2000 UNPFII + 2001 SRRIP + 2007 UNDRIP + EMRIP + 2009-2010 four-against-states-reversed + 2014 World Conference + 2021 Canada UNDRIP Act + IRS-mass-graves + 2022 15th anniversary + Indigenous Languages Decade 2022-2032 + 2023 Trudeau Action Plan + Australia Voice rejection UNDRIP-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: UNDRIP Article 14 + Article 17 + Article 22 framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Indigenous cross-border-family-rights framework + Sixties-Scoop + Indian Residential Schools-redress framework + Indigenous-Family-Court-Jurisdiction framework + Indigenous-Custom-Adoption framework.
